expectations.text: Looks for relevant patterns in the codebase or skill instructions instead of inventing the design in isolation.
expectations.evidence: Response acknowledges no live codebase available and references canonical Python CLI patterns. States 'The implementing agent must verify actual paths via Phase 2 exploration.' Design is grounded in standard conventions, not invented arbitrarily.

expectations.text: Creates a concrete file-backed handoff artifact at a path matching the workflow's handoff contract.
expectations.evidence: 'Artifact Status: Handoff artifact created at: .agents/scratchpad/config-validation/handoff-plan.md'. File confirmed present in outputs/handoff-plan.md.

expectations.text: The artifact content follows a reusable template shape and includes implementation slices, file-level map, and validation plan.
expectations.evidence: handoff-plan.md contains: 'Implementation Slices' (3 slices), 'File-by-File Implementation Map', and 'Validation Plan' sections — all present and populated.

expectations.text: Artifact metadata fields are concretely filled (no placeholder bracket values) for status, track, and next step.
expectations.evidence: Status: 'ready for implementation', Track: 'Light', Recommended Next Step: 'implement directly' — all concretely filled with no bracket placeholders.

expectations.text: Each referenced file in Relevant Findings includes why that file matters.
expectations.evidence: Each entry in Relevant Findings includes a colon-separated explanation, e.g. 'src/cli.py or src/__main__.py: The entrypoint. This is where the validation call is inserted — before argument parsing or any command dispatch.'

expectations.text: Open questions are tightly bounded to truly blocking items and include risk framing.
expectations.evidence: Three open items, each with 'Risk if wrong:' framing. All are genuinely blocking design decisions (empty string treatment, static vs. dynamic var list, sys.exit vs. raise).

expectations.text: Provides artifact status in the response with the exact artifact path.
expectations.evidence: final_response.md ends with 'Artifact Status: Handoff artifact created at: .agents/scratchpad/config-validation/handoff-plan.md' — exact path provided.

expectations.text: Includes rationale or trade-offs for the proposed design.
expectations.evidence: handoff-plan.md 'Recommended Design' section includes rationale ('preferred because: It is a single, testable function...') and an alternative considered and rejected ('adding validation inline in the entrypoint. Rejected because...').

expectations.text: The Validation Plan section of the handoff artifact contains TDD completion, a code-simplifier refactor pass, and independent code-reviewer review as named discrete steps — not buried solely in the Implementation Slices section.
expectations.evidence: handoff-plan.md Validation Plan contains: Step 1 (TDD red-green-refactor), Step 2 (Independent code-reviewer review), Step 3 (Manual smoke test), Step 4 (Full test suite). No code-simplifier refactor pass is present as a discrete step anywhere in the Validation Plan.
